name: kanban

agents.name: designer
agents.description: UI/UX Designer - creates clean designs with clear priority of critical to good-to-have user missions, optimizes information hierarchy and user flows based on mission criticality for the target user segment

agents.name: backend
agents.description: Backend Engineer - builds Python APIs with SQLite databases, designs data models and RESTful endpoints

agents.name: frontend
agents.description: Frontend Engineer - implements React applications with TypeScript, builds reusable components and manages application state

agents.name: devops
agents.description: DevOps Engineer - runs and verifies code, sets up development environments, ensures the application works end-to-end for PM validation

request:
Build a task board application based on the PRD.md specification. Designer should first create the UX design with wireframes and component specs optimized for the target users. Backend should design the API and database schema. Frontend should implement the React components. Coordinate to ensure API contracts align between backend and frontend.

IMPORTANT — Deployment Verification Workflow:
- Every feature milestone MUST end with devops deploying the current project branch using Docker and exposing it via a public tunnel URL.
- After devops reports the deployment URL, post it to user-communication so the user can verify the feature.
- NEVER merge the project branch to main or call project_completed without the user's explicit approval in user-communication. Wait for the user to confirm before proceeding.
- If the user requests changes after testing, create a revision milestone.
